channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden administration announces $500M aid package for Ukraine
item.description: Ukraine will receive another $500 million in military assistance from the United States to help move its stalled war against Russia, the State Department said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House releases national strategy to combat Islamophobia
item.description: The White House has announced what it called the first-ever national strategy to counter Islamophobia.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams meets with Trump border czar: 'We have the same desire' to go after illegal migrant criminals
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams and incoming border czar Tom Homan met Thursday to talk about potential cooperation between local and federal authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House: No evidence of public safety threat from NJ drone sightings
item.description: The White House on Thursday said there is no evidence of a national security or public safety threat after reports of drone sightings in New Jersey, highlighting that the government is working to identify what the mysterious sightings are.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: “I think if President Biden wanted to talk to me about that, I will talk to him about it, but I don’t think I should be giving public advice on the pardon power,” Bill Clinton said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: FBI not involved in Jan. 6 riots: Report
item.description: The FBI reportedly failed to properly canvass field offices for threat information ahead of the riots.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Tide turns in favor of Trump DOD pick Pete Hegseth after Matt Gaetz failure
item.description: Pete Hegseth's luck seems to be turning around in the Senate despite several controversies he has faced as he seeks to become defense secretary in the next Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration makes final diplomatic push for stability across a turbulent Mideast
item.description: Top U.S. officials are in the Middle East pressing for stability in Syria and an end to Israel’s 14-month war in the Gaza Strip in a last-ditch diplomatic push by the outgoing Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Josef Federman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House passes bill to add 66 new federal judgeships, but prospects murky after Biden veto threat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — What was once a bipartisan effort to expand by 66 the number of federal district judgeships across the country passed the House of Representatives on Thursday, though prospects for becoming law are murky after Republicans opted to bring the measure to the floor only after President
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell working from home after tripping at Capitol
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) missed his usual morning speech to open the Senate on Thursday after falling earlier in the week and suffering a minor cut to the face and a sprained wrist.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PA lawmaker demands accountability at UPenn after prof praises Luigi Mangione
item.description: Pennsylvania Congressman Dan Meuser, a potential top contender in the 2026 Pennsylvania gubernatorial sweeps, fired off a letter to UPenn overnight calling for the firing of a professor who praised Luigi Mangione.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House sends Biden bill adding judicial seats after veto threat
item.description: The House approved a bill Thursday that would create dozens of additional judicial seats over the next several years, sending it to President Biden’s desk after his administration levied a veto threat.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teacher sues Beverly Hills school district for racism after Trump win
item.description: The lawyer told NewsNation there were several specific incidents, including a video showing students mocking Black students in the school cafeteria.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Two lawmakers race to expand gun laws
item.description: Concerns about gun violence in St. Pete come as State Representative Dr. Joel Rudman and State Senator Randy Fine juggle gun laws in Tallahassee amid running for Congressional districts 1 and 6.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump, team trail Fauci on trust as medical information source: Survey
item.description: The latest poll from Axios/Ipsos American Health Index shows that 45 percent of Americans say they either have “a great deal” or “a fair amount” of trust in information regarding health topics coming from Fauci.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Cornyn warns Hegseth faces 'unpleasant' confirmation hearing
item.description: Sen. John Cornyn (R-Texas) says Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s pick to head the Department of Defense, faces a nasty Senate confirmation hearing that would likely be uglier than the battle over Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: As session nears, status of a Minnesota lawmaker facing criminal charges remains a flashpoint
item.description: Sen. Nicole Mitchell has upcoming court hearings in a burglary charge ahead of a trial that would start soon after 2025 Legislature gavels in. She’s resisting calls to step down, and a departure would leave the Senate knotted up.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Trump return to DC looms over Kennedy Center Honors
item.description: In a night filled with political moments — including multiple nods to President Joe Biden, a Grateful Dead tribute from former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Congressman Torres blasts crime in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The cold-blooded shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in the heart of Manhattan shocked New Yorkers, including Congressman Ritchie Torres, leaving the lawmaker to pose the question: “What is happening in New York City?” Torres joined PIX on Politics to discuss city crim
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Minneapolis City Council’s budget committee reduces proposed property tax levy for 2025
item.description: Council members, citing concerns about elevated costs for constituents, approved a series of amendments to the mayor’s proposed budget which would reduce the levy hike by 1.4 percent.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: How measles, whooping cough and worse could roar back on RFK Jr.’s watch
item.description: With anti-vaccine activist Robert F. Kennedy Jr. in charge of the nation’s biggest health agency, it wouldn’t take much tampering to enable vaccine-preventable diseases to make a comeback.
item.dc:creator: Arthur Allen, MPR News
